statusLine: EU fuel stocks under strain with 19 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, despite overall averages remaining above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
While EU-wide averages remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum with petrol at 110.1 days, diesel at 100.6 days, and jet fuel at 95.1 days, the distribution reveals significant vulnerabilities across member states. Romania faces the most severe shortages with all three fuel types in critical status, holding just 33.2 days of petrol, 32.4 days of diesel, and 76.4 days of jet fuel. Poland and Luxembourg also show concerning deficits across multiple fuel categories.

The jet fuel sector appears particularly under pressure, with 18 of 27 countries holding critical or warning-level reserves. This widespread shortage in aviation fuel stocks could impact both commercial aviation and military readiness. Diesel reserves show similar stress patterns, with 14 countries at critical levels, potentially affecting freight transport and heating in affected regions.

Oil prices reflect ongoing market tensions, with Brent crude at $99.9 per barrel after a dramatic 37.8% surge since late February's Middle East escalation. This price pressure, combined with the depletion of strategic reserves that several countries released during recent supply disruptions, has created a challenging environment for stock replenishment. The wide price variation across member states—from Malta's €1.34 per liter to Netherlands' €2.39—suggests differing national strategies and supply chain pressures.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries hold critical reserves in at least one fuel type, indicating widespread supply strain despite adequate overall averages
Jet fuel reserves show the most severe shortages, with 18 countries at critical or warning levels, potentially impacting aviation operations
Romania faces the most acute crisis with all fuel types critically low: 33.2 days petrol, 32.4 days diesel, 76.4 days jet fuel
Brent crude has surged 37.8% since late February to $99.9/barrel, creating cost pressures for stock replenishment
Diesel shortages affect 14 countries at critical levels, risking disruption to freight transport and heating supplies
